programmes.show_description: An extended edition of the show with Jimmy Carr, Sean Lock and Jon Richardson, and their guests, Dr Christian Jessen, Lorraine Kelly, Sarah Millican and Tom Rosenthal.
An extended edition of the show with Jimmy Carr, Sean Lock, Jon Richardson and guests Louie Spence, Joe Wilkinson, Elis James and Shappi Khorsandi tackling the week's hot topics.
Stacey Solomon, Darren Harriott, Thomas Skinner and Sarah Keyworth join host Jimmy Carr and team captains Rob Beckett and Katherine Ryan for more topical comedy.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
This episode follows Dippin' Dots, which sells cryogenically frozen beads of ice cream. CEO Scott Fischer wants to see if his empire has the manpower to cope with expansion.
Anytime Fitness is a global firm, and it's the job of brand president Stacy Anderson to keep this gym giant in shape. Are all the employees on board with the planned changes?
In London, Elorm and Mimi prepare an Afro-fusion menu. Kiri and Dale serve up the unexpected. And Serge and Eleni's Greek-Cypriot night features their unique food Olympics.
In East Anglia, Chris and Debbie host a night of showbiz glitz and glamour. Jake and Desiree serve up Caribbean cuisine. And can Daisy and Will get through their evening without arguing?
...Selling: Greg and Kristin have no time for house hunting. Cue Jonathan and Drew, whose next mission is to find a six-bedroom home on a restricted budget. Can it be done?
The One in the Spanish Sea: Carl aims to marry Rebecca on the Med but she has expectations for a high-class affair. Will Carl's plans make a splash and make Rebecca swoon with delight?
The One with All the Pigs: Billy believes the sty's the limit as he plans a pig-themed wedding involving a world-record parade of porkers. Will bride Steph kick up a stink at Billy's plans?
Gary Findley, CEO of Restoration 1, a self-described 'Redneck CEO' from Texas, goes undercover to ensure that there are no flaws in the company's playbook.
Paul Damico, the former president and CEO of Round Table Pizza, goes undercover to learn ways to expand the West Coast pizza franchise eastward and attract a younger clientele.
Chappy's: Gordon visits Chappy's in Tennessee, where the chef and owner's careless methods and less-than-appetising food have hurt his restaurant's local reputation.
Amy's Baking Company: At Amy's Baking Company in Scottsdale, Arizona, Gordon Ramsay meets a husband and wife team with a shocking contempt for customer feedback.
...and Selling: Brand new series: Lori's a self-proclaimed hoarder, making her and Michael's property tough to sell. The bros must clear the path to start a cutthroat clean up.
T-Bone with Stakes: Captain Glenn has to act when a guest is injured. And Gary is confused when his advances to Mads aren't reciprocated.
Water, sand and beach balls contribute a seaside feel to this episode, but the jovial tone is soured by one contestant's flagrant cheating.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Phil Wang take on guest captain Russell Howard and Roisin Conaty. Nick Helm joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and David O'Doherty take on guest captain Johnny Vegas and Jamie Laing. Holly Walsh joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
The Girlfriend: Adam finally brings a girlfriend home for dinner, but things get tricky when the family suddenly have to look after eight-year-old Katie, who has a crush on Adam.
The Fox: Dad is secretly keeping a dead fox in the outside freezer because he wants to get it stuffed. But when Mum needs to use the freezer, can he hide the animal from her?
Jay deals with a couple who are obsessed with getting cheap tattoos on holiday. Lou sorts out Thom, who has a rude tribute to one of his friends on his rear end.
Water, sand and beach balls contribute a seaside feel to this episode, but the jovial tone is soured by one contestant's flagrant cheating.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Phil Wang take on guest captain Russell Howard and Roisin Conaty. Nick Helm joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
TGI Fridays is one of the biggest restaurant chains. CEO Ray Blanchette wants to re-energise the brand, so he goes undercover to find out what staff think of his changes.
Xavier Mufraggi, the CEO of Club Med North America, goes undercover, disguised as a laid-back surfer, in his own resorts, to see the impact that his growth plans are having.
In Essex, sports car manufacturers Charlotte and Neil gear up for some tip-top cuisine. Newly-weds Luke and Alice plan a panto theme. And Hardeep and Sareena host a 'Bhangra Bonanza'.
In Aberdeen, Leanne and Calvin's Ibizan feast competes with Gail and Iain's posh nosh, and Becca and Grant's vegetarian fare, in a week featuring spooky stories and a near-marital-meltdown!
...Selling: Sunita and David's time constraints mean it's not ideal when Jonathan finds a dreadful discovery. Sunita realises the sacrifices she must make to buy a cheaper home.
The Aussie Soap One: Australia-born Adam is taking a risk planning an '80s Aussie soap' wedding when his bride-to-be Rachel is hoping for something classy and sophisticated...
The One Under Edinburgh: Jack plans a haunting, horror-themed wedding, while his bride-to-be Becka dreams of a traditional Scottish affair. Will Jack's concept be a screaming success?
Warren Boone, the chief people officer of restaurant chain Coco's Bakery, steps off the corporate ladder to get a taste of life on the company's front line.
Mayor of Fontana, Acquanetta Warren, goes undercover in the Californian city to spend time with sewer workers and the fire brigade to see how the city is recovering from Covid.
Return to Amy's Baking Company: Gordon revisits the most contentious restaurant he's ever tried to rescue - Amy's Baking Company, in Scottsdale, Arizona.
Pantaleone's: In Colorado, Gordon visits Italian restaurant Pantaleone's, whose stubborn owner Pete claims that he makes 'the best pizza in Denver'. Sadly, Gordon doesn't agree.
...and Selling: Brand new series: Nyahsa and Hezekiyah's hopes of fixing up their home quickly turned into a nightmare. Can Jonathan and Drew dream up an innovative solution?
Hurricane Bonnie: Captain Glenn reprimands the crew over recent slip-ups. And Chef Ileisha is determined to prove she can complete a 10-course dinner.
There's eraser eating, camera wrecking and caravan crushing in the final episode of the series. Which of our intrepid comics will emerge triumphant?
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Joe Wilkinson take on Jon Richardson and Jayde Adams. David Baddiel joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With maths whizz Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and David Mitchell take on guest captain Joe Wilkinson and Roisin Conaty. Tom Allen joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Mr Morris Returns: The family are distraught when Grandma gets engaged to Mr Morris, her horrible 85-year-old boyfriend. And Jim collects money for charity in full dog costume.
The Anniversary: Mum and Dad's anniversary is ruined when Dad paints a picture of Mum that looks like Margaret Thatcher. Jim goes sleep-walking.
